primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex -- Official Journal of the European Union

published: ISO 9001:2015 -- Quality management systems (nonconformity and corrective action)
upcoming: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for AI incident reporting (expected Q4 2026)

obligations.source_quote: Providers of high-risk AI systems placed on the Union market shall report any serious incident to the market surveillance authorities of the Member States where that incident occurred.

decomposed_atoms.description: Must report any serious incident to market surveillance authorities
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL report any serious incident to the market surveillance authorities of the Member States where that incident occurred

obligations.source_quote: The report referred to in paragraph 1 shall be made immediately after the provider has established a causal link between the AI system and the serious incident or the reasonable likelihood of such a link, and, in any event, not later than 15 days after the provider or, where applicable, the deployer, becomes aware of the serious incident.

decomposed_atoms.description: Report must be made immediately and not later than 15 days after becoming aware
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL report immediately after becoming aware, and not later than 15 days

obligations.source_quote: Notwithstanding paragraph 2 of this Article, in the event of a widespread infringement or a serious incident as defined in Article 3, point (49)(b), the report referred to in paragraph 1 of this Article shall be provided immediately, and not later than two days after the provider or, where applicable, the deployer becomes aware of that incident.

decomposed_atoms.description: Widespread infringement must be reported within 2 days
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL report widespread infringement immediately, not later than 2 days after becoming aware

obligations.source_quote: Notwithstanding paragraph 2, in the event of the death of a person, the report shall be provided immediately after the provider or the deployer has established, or as soon as it suspects, a causal relationship between the high-risk AI system and the serious incident, but not later than 10 days after the date on which the provider or, where applicable, the deployer becomes aware of the serious incident.

decomposed_atoms.description: Incidents involving death must be reported within 10 days
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL report incidents involving death immediately, not later than 10 days after becoming aware

obligations.source_quote: Where necessary to ensure timely reporting, the provider or, where applicable, the deployer, may submit an initial report that is incomplete, followed by a complete report.

decomposed_atoms.description: Provider may submit an initial incomplete report followed by a complete report
decomposed_atoms.requirement: MAY submit an initial incomplete report followed by a complete report

obligations.source_quote: Following the reporting of a serious incident pursuant to paragraph 1, the provider shall, without delay, perform the necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident and the AI system concerned. This shall include a risk assessment of the incident, and corrective action.

decomposed_atoms.description: Must perform necessary investigations without delay after a serious incident
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL perform necessary investigations in relation to the serious incident without delay

decomposed_atoms.description: Investigations must include risk assessment and corrective action
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL include risk assessment of the incident and corrective action

decomposed_atoms.description: Must cooperate with competent authorities and notified body during investigations
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL cooperate with the competent authorities, and where relevant with the notified body concerned, during the investigations

decomposed_atoms.description: Must not alter the AI system in ways affecting cause evaluation without informing authorities
decomposed_atoms.requirement: SHALL NOT perform any investigation which involves altering the AI system in a way which may affect any subsequent evaluation of the causes of the incident, prior to informing the competent authorities
